title: Lotto

jokes.text: Snowboarder, it figures. Do snowboarders hate animals? I bet this guy didn't leave his weed in the car.
jokes.setup: Stereotypical snowboarder irresponsibly left dog in car
jokes.punchline: But would never leave weed - priorities completely backwards
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation with good escalation from general stereotype to specific drug reference

jokes.text: Here, I'm going to get in my car. When I start dying, I will honk the horn three times. That means save the dog.
jokes.setup: Argument about whether dog is in danger from heat
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate death signal system to prove a point
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation with perfect specificity (three honks) that makes it funnier

jokes.text: Dwight, at least aim it. [trying to give water to dog through broken window]
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to help dog drink water
jokes.punchline: Dwight is so bad at basic task that Jim has to give aiming instructions
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight incompetence at simple tasks, well-integrated character moment

jokes.text: Don't try to get in on it now, Michael Vick.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to help with dog rescue
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to infamous dog abuser
jokes.explanation: Dark humor reference with perfect economy - two words create the entire joke

jokes.text: Oscar breaking car window and subsequent chaos with dog
jokes.setup: Debate about dog rescue becomes urgent action
jokes.punchline: Oscar actually breaks window, creating bigger problem
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character choice - Oscar as the rule-follower taking drastic action

jokes.text: Nein. Sitz! Gut.
jokes.setup: Trying to control escaped dog
jokes.punchline: Using German commands like it's a trained police dog
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective absurdist moment with good economy

jokes.text: The warehouse crew won the lottery yesterday. $950,000. And then they quit.
jokes.setup: Casual mention of lottery win
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery of life-changing event and immediate consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurd situation that drives entire episode plot

jokes.text: This is it. This is all on my shoulders. I'm the one who has to tell everyone to get back to work.
jokes.setup: Major workplace crisis with lottery-winning staff quitting
jokes.punchline: Andy making it entirely about himself and his minor responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - making crisis about his minor role

jokes.text: When I worked in the warehouse, I was part of that lotto pool. They won playing my birthday.
jokes.setup: Warehouse crew won lottery
jokes.punchline: They won using Darryl's birthday after he left the pool
jokes.explanation: Devastating personal irony delivered with perfect matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: We're looking at, at least one suicide and one weird sex thing.
jokes.setup: Discussion of group dynamics when people win lottery
jokes.punchline: Creed's matter-of-fact prediction of dark outcomes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - casual prediction of chaos with chilling specificity

jokes.text: Hey, Pam. Let's buy expensive bathrobes and hug.
jokes.setup: Jim's fantasy about what he'd do with lottery money
jokes.punchline: Pam's mockingly domestic version of Jim's fantasy
jokes.explanation: Great married couple dynamic - Pam perfectly capturing Jim's sappy tendencies

jokes.text: I'd either bike to my job at the kayak shop or I'd kayak to my job at the bike shop.
jokes.setup: Jim describing idyllic Maine lifestyle fantasy
jokes.punchline: Perfectly symmetrical but completely impractical job arrangement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural joke with perfect symmetry and Jim's earnest delivery

jokes.text: And then on the weekends, would you hacky sack back to reality and spend time with your wife and kids?
jokes.setup: Jim's increasingly ridiculous Maine fantasy
jokes.punchline: Pam's cutting reality check with specific hippie reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect spouse reality check with specific 'hacky sack' detail that nails Jim's fantasy

jokes.text: Soho's mostly lofts, but okay.
jokes.setup: Pam wants townhouse in Soho
jokes.punchline: Jim's pedantic correction of his wife's fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jim can't help correcting even in fantasy scenarios

jokes.text: Stop. I'm a barista in your fantasy?
jokes.setup: Pam describing romantic fantasy with handsome husband bringing coffee
jokes.punchline: Jim's offense at being reduced to service worker in her dream
jokes.explanation: Funny relationship dynamic - Jim's wounded pride at fantasy job

jokes.text: Well, in your fantasy, we're Stephen King characters.
jokes.setup: Jim complaining about being barista in her fantasy
jokes.punchline: Pam pointing out his Maine fantasy is like horror novel setting
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with specific cultural reference that perfectly skewers Jim's fantasy

jokes.text: Get a divorce.
jokes.setup: Escalating fantasy argument between Jim and Pam
jokes.punchline: Pam's matter-of-fact suggestion to end marriage over fantasy disagreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that escalate the absurdity to maximum level

jokes.text: Obviously, I wouldn't come in till noon. And I wouldn't do anything I didn't want to do. I mean, I'm getting paid $1 a year, okay? You can chill.
jokes.setup: Kevin would work for $1 a year if he won lottery
jokes.punchline: Entitled attitude about not working despite choosing to work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic - completely backwards reasoning delivered with confidence

jokes.text: To down here, like Mr. T. And this would get seriously annoying.
jokes.setup: Andy threatening to change his tone if people don't work
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates pathetic Mr. T impression as intimidation tactic
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment showing his ineffective leadership style

jokes.text: I'm not checking e-mail till lunch. Four hour work week.
jokes.setup: Andy asking Darryl about hiring warehouse workers
jokes.punchline: Darryl's complete work avoidance with business book reference
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Darryl's post-lottery depression manifesting as work avoidance

jokes.text: Jim, how about you? Yeah. I mean, as the strongest person in this office, I guess I should... Okay, no, no. That... You are so not... Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Andy asking Jim to volunteer for warehouse work
jokes.punchline: Jim's obvious sarcasm about being strongest person triggers Pam's reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard Jim sarcasm with good Pam reaction showing their relationship dynamic

jokes.text: Well, guess what? I will not do a good job.
jokes.setup: Kevin being volunteered for warehouse work
jokes.punchline: Kevin's honest preview of his performance with defiant pride
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - honest about incompetence but somehow proud of it

jokes.text: Oh, sorry. I thought it was a guess-your-baby's-birth-weight pool.
jokes.setup: Phyllis signing up for what she thinks is lotto pool
jokes.punchline: Reveals she thought it was about Pam's pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phyllis's obliviousness and pregnancy focus

jokes.text: You really think I'm going to have a 14-pound baby?
jokes.setup: Phyllis's baby weight pool confusion
jokes.punchline: Pam's horrified reaction to Phyllis's weight guess
jokes.explanation: Standard pregnancy humor with good character reaction

jokes.text: They guys did invite me out to celebrate, but I decided to just stay home. Eat a bunch of tacos in my basement.
jokes.setup: Asking if Darryl celebrated with lottery winners
jokes.punchline: Darryl's sad alternative to celebration
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Darryl's depression through specific sad detail

jokes.text: Now it smells like tacos. You can't air out a basement. And taco air is heavy. It settles at the lowest point.
jokes.setup: Darryl ate tacos in his basement
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-scientific explanation of why his basement permanently smells like tacos
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with fake scientific reasoning that makes it funnier

jokes.text: That is not Darryl. I don't know where Darryl is. I suspect, probably, our Darryl is inside of Fat Darryl.
jokes.setup: Andy looking at job applicant who looks like Darryl
jokes.punchline: Andy's theory that regular Darryl is trapped inside larger version
jokes.explanation: Creative visual joke with Andy's characteristic weird logic

jokes.text: Negative. Three hundred boxes for me, zero for you chumps. Deal with it.
jokes.setup: Discussing how to divide warehouse work
jokes.punchline: Dwight's completely impractical macho posturing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character moment showing his competitive nature taken to extreme

jokes.text: What? No Newhart fans? Okay.
jokes.setup: Andy makes reference to classic TV show
jokes.punchline: No one gets his dated reference, Andy gives up
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment showing his tendency toward dated cultural references

jokes.text: Glenn is starting one of those fat camps where he steals your kid in the middle of the night. Madge and a couple other guys might start a strip club. But on a boat. And Hide is investing in an energy drink for Asian homosexuals.
jokes.setup: What lottery winners are doing with their money
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre and specific business ventures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity with perfect specificity - each idea gets weirder

jokes.text: Maybe grab a coffee, or if there's any donuts out, you can split one. You know, they're for everybody, so people get fussy. You know what? Just have a donut.
jokes.setup: Andy dismissing job candidates
jokes.punchline: Overthinking donut sharing etiquette while people wait for jobs
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Andy's social awkwardness and poor priorities

jokes.text: Grunting is scientifically proven to add more power. Ask any female tennis player, or her husband.
jokes.setup: Dwight grunting while moving boxes
jokes.punchline: Bizarre explanation with oddly specific tennis reference
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight pseudo-science with unexpected specific detail about husbands

jokes.text: Don't just take the first job that comes your way. 'Cause next thing you know, it's 10 years later, and you're still there. You could write your obituary tomorrow. It's not going to change.
jokes.setup: Interviewing job candidates
jokes.punchline: Darryl's brutally honest description of soul-crushing work life
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor perfectly capturing Darryl's post-lottery depression and existential dread

jokes.text: I've never been lucky. And I'm not talking about the lottery. I'm talking about stuff like developing a soy allergy at 35. Who gets a soy allergy at 35? And why is soy in everything?
jokes.setup: Toby talking about bad luck
jokes.punchline: Specific, relatable complaint about adult-onset food allergy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby moment - mundane complaint that's oddly specific and relatable

jokes.text: Everyone wants to be rich, but nobody wants to work for it. You came in at 10:30 today, right?
jokes.setup: Angela criticizing people buying lottery tickets
jokes.punchline: Kevin's immediate hypocrisy callout
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline with perfect character dynamics - Angela's judgment met with Kevin's facts

jokes.text: Is this a joke? No. Not joking. This is real. Painfully real, what is happening right now.
jokes.setup: Job interview going badly with confusion about licenses
jokes.punchline: Andy's breakdown into existential despair about the interview
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Andy's frustration boiling over into self-aware despair

jokes.text: Well, they whipped people, which was helpful. But you're right.
jokes.setup: Comparing their work to building pyramids
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact acknowledgment that whipping would help efficiency
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Jim's characteristic deadpan delivery of inappropriate thought

jokes.text: Like baboons or elephants.
jokes.setup: Discussing warehouse workers' 'physical intelligence'
jokes.punchline: Jim's comparison to animals with different intelligence types
jokes.explanation: Mildly offensive joke that doesn't quite land, feels more mean than funny

jokes.text: When I was a kid, my sisters used to butter me up and slide me across the linoleum floor of the kitchen.
jokes.setup: Looking for efficient ways to move boxes
jokes.punchline: Kevin's bizarre childhood memory as workplace solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kevin moment - perfectly bizarre personal anecdote with vivid imagery

jokes.text: I like it a lot, but they hate it, so drop it.
jokes.setup: Kevin keeps pushing his butter sliding idea
jokes.punchline: Jim's honest assessment that he likes Kevin's weird idea but it's not happening
jokes.explanation: Good Jim moment showing his diplomatic but honest nature

jokes.text: Then I think you should fire me. I'm not going to fire you. Yeah, just put me out of my misery.
jokes.setup: Darryl failing at job interviews
jokes.punchline: Darryl's dramatic request to be fired like a wounded animal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Darryl's depression manifesting as workplace drama

jokes.text: Who's the most jacked guy in all of Scranton? Like your wildest fantasy guy? Bulk or definition? Definition. Bruce Kenward. He hangs out at Planet Fitness.
jokes.setup: Looking for strong warehouse workers
jokes.punchline: Oscar's detailed knowledge of Scranton's most attractive men
jokes.explanation: Good character moment playing on Oscar's knowledge of attractive men

jokes.text: But Dean got fixated on his calves, and, uh... And his triceps went to hell.
jokes.setup: Discussing strongest men in Scranton
jokes.punchline: Oscar's detailed knowledge of specific muscle group developments
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Oscar's surprisingly detailed fitness knowledge

jokes.text: Because I'm never going to act like that, even in your fantasy. Nope, you're doing a great job of it in my fantasy right now.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam arguing about compromise in fantasies
jokes.punchline: Jim pointing out Pam is being difficult even while claiming she wouldn't be
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about their real argument proving Jim's point

jokes.text: Hey, idiot, what did Erin want again? A hot chocolate tea.
jokes.setup: Michael asking what Erin wanted to drink
jokes.punchline: Nate's confused combination of hot chocolate and tea
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Nate's confusion about basic concepts

jokes.text: Also, FYI, I don't technically have a hearing problem. But sometimes, when there's a lot of noises occurring at the same time, I'll hear them as one big jumble. Again, it's not that I can't hear. Because that's false, I can. Um, I just can't distinguish between everything I'm hearing.
jokes.setup: Job interview with Nate
jokes.punchline: Nate's elaborate explanation of what is clearly a hearing problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment with perfect absurdist logic - denying obvious truth with elaborate explanation

jokes.text: So sadly, it's the best idea on the table. Exactly. Hey, I think we're ready to get this... Jim? Is he okay? He'll be fine.
jokes.setup: Kevin's butter sliding plan being implemented
jokes.punchline: Jim apparently getting hurt during the attempt, others casually dismissing it
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback to Kevin's story with casual dismissal of Jim's injury

jokes.text: Would you just fire me, man? Because you didn't win the lottery?
jokes.setup: Darryl's continued depression and poor performance
jokes.punchline: Andy's direct confrontation about the real issue
jokes.explanation: Good character confrontation that addresses the episode's central conflict

jokes.text: Make me manager or fire me. The job was mine, Andy. Everyone said it was mine.
jokes.setup: Darryl's ultimatum to Andy
jokes.punchline: Reveals deeper resentment about being passed over for promotion
jokes.explanation: Important character moment revealing true source of Darryl's frustration

jokes.text: Also, Darryl, FYI, I already told this to Andy, but you should probably know I technically don't have a hearing problem. It's just when there's a lot of noises... Nate. Please.
jokes.setup: Tense confrontation between Darryl and Andy
jokes.punchline: Nate's oblivious repetition of hearing problem explanation at worst possible time
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback timing - Nate's obliviousness interrupting serious moment

jokes.text: He didn't die, his brain died.
jokes.setup: Discussion about what happened to Deangelo
jokes.punchline: Darryl's morbid but technically accurate correction
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor with great economy - five words that are both funny and accurate

jokes.text: My future's not going to be determined by seven little white lotto balls. It's going to be determined by two big black balls. I control my destiny.
jokes.setup: Darryl's realization about taking control of his future
jokes.punchline: Unintentional sexual innuendo in motivational speech
jokes.explanation: Good accidental innuendo that works because Darryl is being sincere

jokes.text: Tell them why it's called señor Loadenstein. Doesn't matter what the name is. Señor Loadenstein, that's stupid. Tell them why it's called that, Jim.
jokes.setup: Kevin proud of their warehouse contraption name
jokes.punchline: Kevin forcing Jim to explain ridiculous name while Jim tries to avoid it
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Kevin's pride in terrible name and Jim's embarrassment

jokes.text: Porque es muy rápido.
jokes.setup: Forced to explain why contraption is called Señor Loadenstein
jokes.punchline: Jim's deadpan Spanish explanation of the stupid name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim moment - giving in to Kevin's pressure with perfect deadpan Spanish

jokes.text: I already won the lottery. I was born in the US of A, baby. And as backup, I have a Swiss passport.
jokes.setup: Everyone obsessing over lottery
jokes.punchline: Creed's patriotic perspective with mysterious Swiss connection
jokes.explanation: Good Creed moment with patriotic sentiment and mysterious backup plan

jokes.text: I would spend a lot of time launching my true crime podcast, 'The Flenderson Files.' Duh-buh-bum.
jokes.setup: What Toby would do if he won lottery
jokes.punchline: Self-aggrandizing podcast name with his own sound effects
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby moment - self-important true crime podcast with dramatic sound effect

jokes.text: We're going to live in a stunning pre-war brownstone at the top of a mountain. Right, it's city and country combined.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam's compromise fantasy location
jokes.punchline: Physically impossible architectural scenario presented as reasonable compromise
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist compromise that shows how their fantasy arguments resolved absurdly

jokes.text: It's too bad the schools are terrible, but what are you going to do about that? What are you going to do?
jokes.setup: Perfect fantasy life compromise
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of major flaw in their impossible dream scenario
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat showing even in fantasy compromises, problems remain

tmdb_overview: The entire warehouse staff quits after winning the lotto, leaving Andy and Darryl to scramble for replacements while several of the office workers get a taste of the warehouse life.
